114	
115	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — corepresentability of the sections functor
116	
117	The sections functor `Γ(V, -)` on `X.Modules` is corepresented by the Form-B object
118	`jShriekOU V`.  Consequently its right derived functors agree, degreewise, with those of the
119	Hom-functor `Hom(jShriekOU V, -)` — the homological half of the identification
120	`Rᵠ Γ(V, -) ≅ Ext^q(jShriekOU V, -)` (the deep "Bridge (1)/(2)" connecting the higher-direct-image
121	presheaf homology to absolute cohomology). -/
122	
123	/-- **Naturality of `jShriekOU_homEquiv` in the coefficient sheaf** (reproduced here; the version
124	in `AbsoluteCohomology.lean` is `private`). Composing a map `jShriekOU V ⟶ F₁` with `g : F₁ ⟶ F₂`
125	corresponds, under corepresentability, to applying `g` on sections over `V`. -/
126	private lemma jShriekOU_homEquiv_nat (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
127	    {F₁ F₂ : X.Modules} (h : jShriekOU V ⟶ F₁) (g : F₁ ⟶ F₂) :
128	    jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₂ (h ≫ g)
129	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map g).app (op V)))
130	          (jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₁ h) := by
131	  have hs : sheafificationHomAddEquiv V F₂ (h ≫ g)
132	        = sheafificationHomAddEquiv V F₁ h ≫ (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map g :=
133	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
134	        (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).homEquiv_naturality_right h g
135	  change freeYonedaHomEquiv V _ (sheafificationHomAddEquiv V F₂ (h ≫ g)) = _
136	  rw [hs, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, PresheafOfModules.comp_app, ModuleCat.comp_apply]
137	  rfl
138	
139	/-- `Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X` is additive (it is the additive composite
140	`forget ⋙ restrictScalars (𝟙)`). Project-local instance so `sectionsFunctor` is additive. -/
141	noncomputable instance toPresheafOfModules_additive :
142	    (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).Additive :=
143	  inferInstanceAs (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
144	    PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).Additive
145	
146	/-- The sections functor `Γ(V,-) : X.Modules ⥤ Ab` is additive. Project-local instance (the flat
147	triple composite does not infer additivity automatically). -/
148	noncomputable instance sectionsFunctor_additive (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
149	    (AlgebraicGeometry.sectionsFunctor V).Additive := by
150	  unfold AlgebraicGeometry.sectionsFunctor
151	  haveI i2 : (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
152	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (op V)).Additive :=
153	    Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _
154	  exact Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _
155	
156	/-- **The sections functor `Γ(V,-)` is corepresented by `jShriekOU V`.** A natural isomorphism
157	`sectionsFunctor V ≅ preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op (jShriekOU V))`, assembled from the additive
158	corepresentability bijection `jShriekOU_homEquiv` and its naturality. Project-local: the functorial
159	upgrade of `jShriekOU_homEquiv`, the geometric input to the absolute-cohomology bridge. -/
160	noncomputable def sectionsFunctorCorepIso (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
161	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionsFunctor V ≅ preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op (jShriekOU V)) :=
162	  NatIso.ofComponents
163	    (fun F => (AddEquiv.toAddCommGrpIso (jShriekOU_homEquiv V F)).symm)
164	    (by
165	      intro F₁ F₂ g
166	      ext s
167	      simp only [AddEquiv.toAddCommGrpIso, comp_apply, Iso.symm_hom, AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom,
168	        AddEquiv.coe_toAddMonoidHom]
169	      rw [AddEquiv.symm_apply_eq]
170	      change (ConcreteCategory.hom ((AlgebraicGeometry.sectionsFunctor V).map g)) s
171	        = jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₂ ((jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₁).symm s ≫ g)
172	      rw [jShriekOU_homEquiv_nat V ((jShriekOU_homEquiv V F₁).symm s) g, AddEquiv.apply_symm_apply]
173	      rfl)
174	
175	/-- **Right derived functors transport along a natural isomorphism of additive functors.**
176	Project-local: assembled from `NatTrans.rightDerived` of `τ.hom`/`τ.inv` and functoriality
177	(`NatTrans.rightDerived_comp`, `NatTrans.rightDerived_id`). -/
178	noncomputable def rightDerivedNatIso {C D : Type*} [Category C] [Category D] [Abelian C]
179	    [HasInjectiveResolutions C] [Abelian D] {F G : C ⥤ D} [F.Additive] [G.Additive]
180	    (τ : F ≅ G) (n : ℕ) : F.rightDerived n ≅ G.rightDerived n where
181	  hom := NatTrans.rightDerived τ.hom n
182	  inv := NatTrans.rightDerived τ.inv n
183	  hom_inv_id := by rw [← NatTrans.rightDerived_comp, τ.hom_inv_id, NatTrans.rightDerived_id]
184	  inv_hom_id := by rw [← NatTrans.rightDerived_comp, τ.inv_hom_id, NatTrans.rightDerived_id]
185	
186	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `Ext` transport along the spectrum equivalence (Need #1)
187	
188	The whole-scheme spectrum isomorphism `U ≅ Spec Γ(U,𝒪)` (`Scheme.isoSpec`, a genuine iso because
189	`U` is affine) induces an *equivalence* of module categories `U.Modules ≌ (Spec Γ U).Modules`,
190	assembled from `Scheme.Modules.pushforward` along the iso and its coherences. An equivalence functor
191	is fully faithful, exact, and preserves injective objects, so
192	`Functor.mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects` upgrades `Ext.mapExactFunctor` along it
193	to an *isomorphism* of `Ext`-groups. This transports the residual general-affine-open Serre
194	vanishing for `U` to the `Spec`-developed `affine_serre_vanishing_general_open`.
195	
196	The pushforward-of-an-iso construction is generic in the scheme isomorphism `φ : X ≅ Y`. -/
197	
198	/-- **A scheme isomorphism induces an equivalence of module categories.** For `φ : X ≅ Y`, the
199	pushforward `Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.hom` is an equivalence with quasi-inverse the pushforward
200	along `φ.inv`; the unit/counit are supplied by the
201	`pushforwardId`/`pushforwardComp`/`pushforwardCongr` coherences. Project-local: Mathlib packages the
202	pushforward functor and its coherences but not the equivalence assembled from an iso (the genuine
203	equivalence used to transport `Ext` for Need #1). -/
204	noncomputable def Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y) :
205	    X.Modules ≌ Y.Modules :=
206	  CategoryTheory.Equivalence.mk
207	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.hom) (Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.inv)
208	    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId X).symm ≪≫ Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr φ.hom_inv_id.symm ≪≫
209	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp φ.hom φ.inv).symm)
210	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp φ.inv φ.hom ≪≫ Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr φ.inv_hom_id ≪≫
211	      Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId Y)
212	
213	/-- The functor of `pushforwardEquivOfIso φ` is (defeq) the additive `pushforward φ.hom`. -/
214	noncomputable instance pushforwardEquivOfIso_functor_additive {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y) :
215	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.Additive :=
216	  inferInstanceAs (Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.hom).Additive
217	
218	/-- **`Ext` transport along a scheme isomorphism.** For `φ : X ≅ Y` and `A B : X.Modules`, the
219	equivalence `pushforwardEquivOfIso φ` (whose functor is fully faithful, exact, and injective-object
220	preserving) upgrades the additive map `Ext.mapExactFunctor` to an isomorphism of `Ext`-groups
221	`Ext A B n ≃+ Ext (φ_* A) (φ_* B) n`, via `Functor.mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects`.
222	Project-local: the `Ext`-isomorphism half of Need #1 (the spectrum-equivalence transport). -/
223	noncomputable def Scheme.Modules.pushforwardExtAddEquiv {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y)
224	    [EnoughInjectives X.Modules] (A B : X.Modules) (n : ℕ) :
225	    CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext A B n ≃+
226	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj A)
227	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj B) n :=
228	  AddEquiv.ofBijective ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.mapExtAddHom A B n)
229	    (Functor.mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects
230	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor A B n)
231	
232	/-- **Need #1 — `Ext` transport along the whole-scheme spectrum equivalence.** For an affine scheme
233	`U`, the canonical iso `U ≅ Spec Γ(U,𝒪)` (`Scheme.isoSpec`) induces, for every `q`, an isomorphism
234	`Ext^q_U(A, B) ≅ Ext^q_{Spec Γ U}(φ_* A, φ_* B)`. In particular the residual `Ext` over `U` vanishes
235	iff its transport over `Spec Γ U` does. Project-local target of `lem:modules_isoSpec_ext_transport`.
236	
237	The remaining identification of the transported arguments — `φ_*(jShriekOU V) ≅ jShriekOU V'` for
238	`V'` the (affine) image open, and quasi-coherence of `φ_* H` — is handed off (see the task-result
239	file); it is orthogonal to this transport and is the geometric naturality of `j_!` under a scheme
240	iso. -/
241	noncomputable def modulesIsoSpecExtTransport (U : Scheme.{u}) [IsAffine U]
242	    [EnoughInjectives U.Modules] (A B : U.Modules) (n : ℕ) :
243	    CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext A B n ≃+
244	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext
245	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso U.isoSpec).functor.obj A)
246	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso U.isoSpec).functor.obj B) n :=
247	  Scheme.Modules.pushforwardExtAddEquiv U.isoSpec A B n
248	
249	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `Ext`-vanishing ⟹ coyoneda right-derived vanishing
250	
251	The homological-algebra half of the relative-affine vanishing argument (Bridge (1)/(2) remainder).
252	The `q`-th right derived functor of the additive Hom-functor `Hom(P,-) = preadditiveCoyoneda(op P)`,
253	evaluated at `H`, is computed (via the chosen injective resolution `I•` of `H`,
254	`InjectiveResolution.isoRightDerivedObj`) as the homology at degree `q` of the cochain complex of
255	abelian groups `n ↦ (P ⟶ Iⁿ)`.  By `InjectiveResolution.extMk` / `extMk_eq_zero_iff`, that homology
256	*is* `Ext P H q`: a degree-`q` cocycle is a map `f : P ⟶ Iq` with `f ≫ d = 0`, and it is a coboundary
257	iff the class `extMk f` vanishes in `Ext`.  Hence if every class of `Ext P H q` vanishes (`q ≥ 1`),
258	the Hom-complex is exact at `q` and the right-derived object is a zero object.  Project-local: Mathlib's
259	`Ext` is built on the derived category, not on `Functor.rightDerived`, so this bridge is absent. -/
260	
261	/-- The differential of the Hom-cochain-complex `n ↦ (P ⟶ Iⁿ)` (the image of a cochain complex `K`
262	under `preadditiveCoyoneda(op P)`) acts by post-composition with the differential of `K`. -/
263	private lemma preadditiveCoyoneda_mapHomologicalComplex_d_apply {C : Type*} [Category C]
264	    [Preadditive C] (P : C) (K : CochainComplex C ℕ) (i j : ℕ) (g : P ⟶ K.X i) :

271	`Ext P H q` is zero (`q ≥ 1`), then `(Hom(P,-)).rightDerived q` is a zero object at `H`. The

274	lemma isZero_coyoneda_rightDerived_of_forall_ext_eq_zero {C : Type*} [Category C]
275	    [Abelian C] [HasInjectiveResolutions C] [CategoryTheory.HasExt C] (P H : C) (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q)
276	    (hExt : ∀ e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext P H q, e = 0) :
277	    IsZero (((preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op P)).rightDerived q).obj H) := by
278	  obtain ⟨p, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero hq.ne'
279	  refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ ((injectiveResolution H).isoRightDerivedObj
280	    (preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op P)) (p + 1))
281	  set R := injectiveResolution H with hR

283	  rw [HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff' _ p (p + 1) (p + 2) (by simp) (by simp),
284	    ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff]
285	  intro x₂ hx₂
286	  have hf : x₂ ≫ R.cocomplex.d (p + 1) (p + 2) = 0 := by
287	    rw [← preadditiveCoyoneda_mapHomologicalComplex_d_apply P R.cocomplex (p + 1) (p + 2) x₂]
288	    exact hx₂
289	  obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := (R.extMk_eq_zero_iff x₂ (p + 2) rfl hf p rfl).mp (hExt _)
290	  refine ⟨g, ?_⟩

292	  exact preadditiveCoyoneda_mapHomologicalComplex_d_apply P R.cocomplex p (p + 1) g

300	    [HasInjectiveResolutions C] : EnoughInjectives C where
301	  presentation X := by
302	    obtain ⟨R⟩ := (HasInjectiveResolutions.out X).out
303	    exact ⟨{ J := R.cocomplex.X 0, injective := R.injective 0, f := R.ι.f 0

306	/-- **An isomorphism in the first `Ext`-argument transfers subsingletons.** If `Ext B Y q` is a
307	subsingleton and `φ : A ≅ B`, then so is `Ext A Y q` (every class is the `φ.hom`-precomposite of its
308	`φ.inv`-precomposite, which vanishes). Project-local: the contravariant `Ext`-functoriality packaged
309	for the Serre-vanishing transport along the `jShriekOU` transport iso. -/
310	lemma subsingleton_ext_of_iso_fst {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C] [CategoryTheory.HasExt C]
311	    {A B : C} (φ : A ≅ B) (Y : C) (q : ℕ)
312	    [Subsingleton (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext B Y q)] :
313	    Subsingleton (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext A Y q) := by
314	  refine ⟨fun a b => ?_⟩
315	  suffices h : ∀ z : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext A Y q, z = 0 by rw [h a, h b]
316	  intro z
317	  have hw : (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ φ.inv).comp z (zero_add q) = 0 := Subsingleton.elim _ _
318	  have hz : z = (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ φ.hom).comp
319	      ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ φ.inv).comp z (zero_add q)) (zero_add q) := by
320	    rw [Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀_assoc, φ.hom_inv_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp]
321	  rw [hz, hw, Abelian.Ext.comp_zero]

323	/-- **Serre `Ext`-vanishing over an affine scheme via the spectrum transport.** Let `U` be a scheme,
324	`φ : U ≅ Spec R` a scheme isomorphism, `V ⊆ U` an open and `H` an `𝒪_U`-module.  If the pushforward
325	`Φ(j_!𝒪_V)` of the corepresenting object is isomorphic to `j_!𝒪_{V'}` for an *affine* open
326	`V' ⊆ Spec R`, and `Φ H` is quasi-coherent, then every class of `Ext^q_U(j_!𝒪_V, H)` vanishes for
327	`q ≥ 1`.  Assembled from `pushforwardExtAddEquiv` (the spectrum transport), the contravariant
328	`Ext`-transfer `subsingleton_ext_of_iso_fst`, and the now-unconditional
329	`affine_serre_vanishing_general_open` on `Spec R`.  Project-local: the Bridge (2) Serre-vanishing
330	leaf of the open-immersion acyclicity, parameterized over the geometric transport data so that the
331	residual `jShriekOU`/quasi-coherence transport sub-lemmas are isolated. -/
332	lemma ext_jShriekOU_eq_zero_of_specIso {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (U : Scheme.{u})
333	    [HasInjectiveResolutions U.Modules] (φ : U ≅ Spec R) (V : U.Opens) (H : U.Modules)
334	    (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) (V' : (Spec R).Opens) (hV' : IsAffineOpen V')
335	    (hjt : (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj (jShriekOU V) ≅ jShriekOU V')
336	    (hqc : ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj H).IsQuasicoherent)
337	    (e : Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU V) H q) : e = 0 := by
338	  haveI : EnoughInjectives U.Modules := enoughInjectives_of_hasInjectiveResolutions
339	  set Φ := Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ with hΦ
340	  haveI : EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules := EnoughInjectives.of_equivalence Φ.inverse
341	  haveI : (Φ.functor.obj H).IsQuasicoherent := hqc
342	  haveI hsub0 : Subsingleton (Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU V') (Φ.functor.obj H) q) :=
343	    ⟨fun a b => (affine_serre_vanishing_general_open _ V' hV' q hq a).trans
344	      (affine_serre_vanishing_general_open _ V' hV' q hq b).symm⟩
345	  haveI hsub1 : Subsingleton (Abelian.Ext (Φ.functor.obj (jShriekOU V)) (Φ.functor.obj H) q) :=
346	    subsingleton_ext_of_iso_fst hjt (Φ.functor.obj H) q
347	  have htr := Scheme.Modules.pushforwardExtAddEquiv φ (jShriekOU V) H q
348	  exact htr.injective (Subsingleton.elim (htr e) (htr 0))

363	/-- **Higher direct images of an affine open immersion vanish** (part (1) of blueprint
364	`lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp`; Stacks `lemma-relative-affine-vanishing`).
365	
366	For an open immersion `j : U ↪ X` where `U` is affine and `X` is separated, the morphism
367	`j` is affine (since for any affine open `V ⊆ X`, `j⁻¹V = U ∩ V` is affine by separatedness).
368	Therefore `R^q j_* H = 0` for every `q ≥ 1` and every quasi-coherent `O_U`-module `H`.

370	Proof route: by `higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology`, `R^q j_* H` is the
371	sheafification of `V ↦ H^q(j⁻¹V, H)`. For affine `V`, `j⁻¹V = U ∩ V` is affine (`U` affine
372	+ `X` separated), and `affine_serre_vanishing` kills `H^q` for `q ≥ 1`. Since affine opens
373	form a basis, the presheaf homology is locally zero, and the site lemmas
374	`isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective` (in `CechHigherDirectImage.lean`,

376	private lemma isAffineHom_of_affine_separated (j : U ⟶ X) [IsAffine U] [X.IsSeparated] :
377	    IsAffineHom j := by
378	  have hg : IsSeparated (terminal.from X) := Scheme.IsSeparated.isSeparated_terminal_from
379	  have hcomp : IsAffineHom (j ≫ terminal.from X) := by
380	    have he : j ≫ terminal.from X = terminal.from U := terminal.hom_ext _ _
381	    rw [he]; infer_instance
382	  exact IsAffineHom.of_comp j (terminal.from X)

384	/-- The sections-over-`j⁻¹W` functor on `U`-modules, `M ↦ Γ(W, j_* M) = Γ(j⁻¹W, M)`,
385	realised as the composite `pushforward j ⋙ forget ⋙ restrictScalars 𝟙 ⋙ toPresheaf ⋙ eval(W)`.
386	Its `q`-th right derived functor applied to `H` is the absolute cohomology `Hᵠ(j⁻¹W, H)`.
387	Project-local: a named composite so its additivity instance is synthesised once at the top
388	level (the 5-fold composite defeats in-proof instance search). -/
389	noncomputable def pushforwardSectionsFunctor (j : U ⟶ X) (W : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
390	    U.Modules ⥤ AddCommGrpCat :=
391	  Scheme.Modules.pushforward j ⋙ SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
392	    PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj) ⋙
393	    PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
394	    (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W)

397	    (j : U ⟶ X) (W : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : (pushforwardSectionsFunctor j W).Additive := by
398	  unfold pushforwardSectionsFunctor
399	  -- The flat 5-fold composite defeats instance search; build it as an explicit `instAdditiveComp`
400	  -- chain (every individual factor is additive).
401	  haveI hpf : (Scheme.Modules.pushforward j).Additive := inferInstance
402	  haveI i4 : (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
403	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W)).Additive :=
404	    Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _
405	  haveI i3 : (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj) ⋙
406	      PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
407	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W)).Additive :=
408	    Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _
409	  haveI i2 : (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
410	      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj) ⋙
411	      PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
412	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W)).Additive :=
413	    Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _
414	  -- Instance search will not pick up `i2` automatically here, so we pass it explicitly.
415	  exact @CategoryTheory.Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _ _ _ _ _
416	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward j) hpf _ _ _ _ i2

418	theorem higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic [HasInjectiveResolutions U.Modules]
419	    (j : U ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion j] [IsAffine U] [X.IsSeparated]
420	    (H : U.Modules) (hH : H.IsQuasicoherent) (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) :
421	    IsZero (higherDirectImage j q H) := by
422	  -- `j` is an affine morphism (open immersion of an affine into a separated scheme),
423	  -- so the preimage of every affine open `V ⊆ X` is the affine open `j⁻¹V = U ∩ V`.

425	  -- Presheaf description (Stacks 01XJ): `Rᵠ j_* H ≅ sheafify(V ↦ Hᵠ((j_* I•)(V)))`

427	  refine IsZero.of_iso ?_
428	    (higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology j q (injectiveResolution H))
429	  set α : X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ X.ringCatSheaf.obj := 𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj with hα
430	  set P := (pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex j (injectiveResolution H)).homology q with hP
431	  -- Reflect `IsZero` through the faithful, zero-preserving forgetful functor `toSheaf`.
432	  apply Functor.isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms (SheafOfModules.toSheaf X.ringCatSheaf)
433	  -- Transport across the sheafification square
434	  -- `toSheaf ∘ sheafification ≅ presheafToSheaf ∘ toPresheaf`.
435	  refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationCompToSheaf α).app P)
436	  set Q := (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj P with hQ
437	  -- KEY VANISHING (genuine cohomological gap): the presheaf homology `Q` is a *zero object*
438	  -- over every affine open `W ⊆ X`, because `j⁻¹W` is affine (`j` is an affine morphism) and
439	  -- `affine_serre_vanishing` kills `Hᵠ(j⁻¹W, H)` for `q ≥ 1`.

441	      IsZero (Q.obj (Opposite.op W)) := by
442	    intro W hW

444	    -- `Q.obj (op W) ≅ Hᵠ(Γ(W, f_* I•)) = Hᵠ(j⁻¹W, H)`, which vanishes for affine `j⁻¹W`.

446	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W) with hGW

459	      (pushforwardSectionsFunctor j W) q).symm

512	/-- **Composition formula for higher direct images across an open immersion** (pinned
513	declaration; blueprint `lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp`, part (2); Stacks
514	`lemma-relative-affine-vanishing`).
515	
516	Let `j : U ↪ X` be an open immersion of an affine open `U` into a separated scheme `X`,
517	and `f : X ⟶ S` any morphism. For every quasi-coherent `O_U`-module `H` and every `k ≥ 0`:
518	```
519	  R^k f_*(j_* H) ≅ R^k (f ∘ j)_* H.
520	```
521	Proof route: choose an injective resolution `H → I•` in `U.Modules`; apply `j_*`
522	degreewise to get an `f_*`-acyclic resolution `j_* I•` of `j_* H`
523	(`higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic` for `j_*`-acyclicity, extended to
524	`f_*`-acyclicity via the same Serre-vanishing argument on `U ∩ f⁻¹V`); then
525	`Functor.rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution` with `G = (pushforward f)` and the identity
526	`(pushforward f) ∘ (pushforward j) = pushforward (j ≫ f)` give the stated isomorphism. -/
527	noncomputable def higherDirectImage_openImmersion_comp
528	    [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules] [HasInjectiveResolutions U.Modules]
529	    (j : U ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion j] [IsAffine U] [X.IsSeparated]
530	    (f : X ⟶ S) (H : U.Modules) (hH : H.IsQuasicoherent) (k : ℕ) :
531	    higherDirectImage f k ((pushforward j).obj H) ≅
532	      higherDirectImage (j ≫ f) k H := by
533	  haveI : IsAffineHom j := isAffineHom_of_affine_separated j
534	  -- Structural plan (all categorical building blocks below are verified to exist):
535	  --   • `G := Scheme.Modules.pushforward f` is additive and preserves finite limits
536	  --     (`inferInstance`), so `Functor.rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution` applies to it.
537	  --   • The pushed-forward injective resolution `K := (pushforward j).mapHomologicalComplex I•`
538	  --     is a candidate acyclic resolution of `j_* H = (pushforward j).obj H`.
539	  --   • `pushforwardComp j f : pushforward j ⋙ pushforward f ≅ pushforward (j ≫ f)` turns
540	  --     `H^k(G(K))` into `H^k((j ≫ f)_* I•) = R^k (j ≫ f)_* H`.
541	  -- RESIDUAL (genuine cohomological gaps, handed off — each depends on Part (1)'s residual):
542	  --   (a) `K` is exact in positive degrees and resolves `j_* H` (i.e. `j_* I•` is a
543	  --       resolution of `j_* H`) — this is `j_*`-acyclicity, the content of
544	  --       `higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic` applied to each `Iⁿ` and to `H`.

\section{Setup and motivation}

This chapter constructs the higher derived direct images \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) for
\(i \geq 1\) \emph{without appealing to injective resolutions in the category of
sheaves of modules}. The companion development of
Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_HigherDirectImage} defines \(R^i f_*\) as a right
derived functor via injective resolutions, which requires the ambient category of

9479	  For an adjunction \(F \dashv G\) between categories, the covariant representable functor corepresented

9481	  \(A\):

9488	  transported across an equivalence is corepresented on the other side by the inverse-image object.''
9489	\end{lemma}

9494	  \mathlibok

9497	  (\mathcal{C} \to \mathrm{Type})\) is fully faithful; in particular it reflects isomorphisms, so a
9498	  natural isomorphism \(\operatorname{coyoneda}(\mathrm{op}\,A) \cong \operatorname{coyoneda}(\mathrm{op}\,B)\)
9499	  comes from a unique isomorphism \(A \cong B\) (via the preimage of the iso).

9524	  \(A = j_!\mathcal{O}_V\). It suffices, by full faithfulness of \(\operatorname{coyoneda}\)
9525	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:coyoneda_fullyFaithful_mathlib}, which reflects isomorphisms), to produce a natural
9526	  isomorphism of the corepresented functors

9528	  \operatorname{coyoneda}(\mathrm{op}(j_!\mathcal{O}_{\varphi.\mathrm{inv}^{-1}V}))\); the desired
9529	  isomorphism of objects is then its preimage.

9532	  \begin{enumerate}
9533	    \item The equivalence–coyoneda isomorphism of Lemma~\ref{lem:compCoyonedaIso_mathlib}, applied to the
9534	      adjunction underlying \(\Phi\), gives

9536	      \Phi.\mathrm{inverse} \circ \operatorname{coyoneda}(\mathrm{op}\,A)\).
9537	    \item The corepresentability isomorphism of Lemma~\ref{lem:sectionsFunctorCorepIso}, whiskered by the

9539	      \(\operatorname{coyoneda}(\mathrm{op}\,A) \cong \operatorname{sectionsFunctor} V\) (as
9540	      \(\mathrm{Type}\)-valued functors). This rests on the corepresentability of \(\Gamma(V,-)\) by
9541	      \(j_!\mathcal{O}_V\) of Definition~\ref{def:jshriek_ou}.
9542	    \item Sections of a pushforward are, by definition, sections over the preimage open:
9543	      \(\Phi.\mathrm{inverse} \circ \operatorname{sectionsFunctor} V =
9544	      \operatorname{sectionsFunctor}(\varphi.\mathrm{inv}^{-1}V)\), an equality of functors. Reading
9545	      Lemma~\ref{lem:sectionsFunctorCorepIso} backwards at the open \(\varphi.\mathrm{inv}^{-1}V\) then
9546	      identifies this with \(\operatorname{coyoneda}(\mathrm{op}(j_!\mathcal{O}_{\varphi.\mathrm{inv}^{-1}V}))\).

9549	  Lemma~\ref{lem:coyoneda_fullyFaithful_mathlib} reflects it to the stated isomorphism of objects. No
9550	  adjunction-mate transposition or naturality square is computed by hand: the only geometric inputs are
9551	  the two definitional equalities ``corepresented functor \(=\) sections functor'' and ``sections of a
9552	  pushforward \(=\) sections over the preimage''.

9575	\begin{lemma}[Pushforward along an iso commutes with restriction to an open]
9576	  \label{lem:pushforward_commutes_restriction}
9577	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_commutes_restriction}
9578	  % NOTE: build target. The Lean declaration does not exist yet.
9579	  \uses{lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib, lem:restrict_obj_mathlib}
9580	  Let \(\varphi : X \cong Y\) be an isomorphism of schemes,
9581	  \(\Phi = \operatorname{pushforwardEquivOfIso}\varphi\) the induced equivalence of module categories,
9582	  and \(V \subseteq X\) an open with image \(V' = \varphi.\mathrm{inv}^{-1}\,V \subseteq Y\). The
9583	  homeomorphism underlying \(\varphi\) restricts to a homeomorphism \(V \cong V'\), hence to an
9584	  equivalence of the opens-sites \(\operatorname{Opens} V \simeq \operatorname{Opens} V'\); let
9585	  \[
9586	    e : (X.\mathrm{Modules} \!\restriction\! V) \;\simeq\; (Y.\mathrm{Modules} \!\restriction\! V')
9587	  \]
9588	  be the induced equivalence of restricted-module categories obtained from the site-equivalence
9589	  transport of Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib}. Then for every
9590	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(H\) there is a comparison isomorphism
9591	  \[
9592	    e.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\bigl(H.\operatorname{over} V\bigr)
9593	      \;\cong\;
9594	    \bigl(\Phi.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\, H\bigr).\operatorname{over} V',
9595	  \]
9596	  identifying ``restrict to \(V\) then push forward over \(V'\)'' with ``push forward then restrict to
9597	  \(V'\)''.
9598	\end{lemma}
9599	\begin{proof}
9600	  \uses{lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib, lem:restrict_obj_mathlib}
9601	  Both sides are computed open-by-open on the subspace site of \(V'\). For an open
9602	  \(W \subseteq V'\) with preimage \(W_0 = \varphi.\mathrm{hom}^{-1}\,W \subseteq V\), the sections of the
9603	  right-hand restriction are the sections of \(\Phi.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\, H\) over the image
9604	  open \(W\), which — pushforward acting on opens by the inverse-image homeomorphism — are the sections
9605	  of \(H\) over \(W_0\) (the restriction-of-objects identity of Lemma~\ref{lem:restrict_obj_mathlib},
9606	  applied to the open immersions \(V \hookrightarrow X\) and \(V' \hookrightarrow Y\)). The same
9607	  sections compute the left-hand side: the site-equivalence transport
9608	  \(e\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib} relabels the open \(W\) of \(V'\)
9609	  to the corresponding open \(W_0\) of \(V\) and reads off \(H.\operatorname{over} V\) there. The two
9610	  presheaves of sections therefore agree, compatibly with restriction maps, and the comparison is a
9611	  morphism of sheaves of modules over \(V'\); being an isomorphism on every section group it is an
9612	  isomorphism. This is the open-restriction analogue of the \(\operatorname{bind}\) construction's
9613	  per-piece transport, with the homeomorphism-induced opens-site equivalence in place of the
9614	  iterated-slice equivalence.
9615	\end{proof}

9617	\begin{lemma}[Pushforward along an iso preserves quasi-coherence]
9618	  \label{lem:pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh}
9619	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh}
9620	  % NOTE: build target. The Lean declaration does not exist yet.
9621	  \uses{lem:pushforward_commutes_restriction, lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib,
9622	    lem:presentation_map_mathlib, lem:isQuasicoherent_of_coversTop_mathlib,
9623	    lem:nonempty_quasicoherentData_mathlib, lem:presentation_ofIsIso_mathlib,
9624	    lem:isAffineOpen_image_of_iso_mathlib}
9625	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, Section ``Functoriality for quasi-coherent
9626	  % modules'', items (1),(7) and the closing remark on pushforward (read from
9627	  % references/stacks-schemes.tex, L4729--4767).
9628	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "A sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules $\mathcal{F}$ is
9629	  % quasi-coherent if and only if the restriction of $\mathcal{F}$
9630	  % to each affine open $U = \Spec(R)$ is of the form
9631	  % $\widetilde M$ for some $R$-module $M$."
9632	  % "Given a morphism of schemes $f : Y \to X$ the pullback
9633	  % of a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module is a quasi-coherent
9634	  % $\mathcal{O}_Y$-module. See
9635	  % Modules, Lemma \ref{modules-lemma-pullback-quasi-coherent}."
9636	  % "On the other hand, it is in general not the case that the
9637	  % pushforward of a quasi-coherent module is quasi-coherent.
9638	  % Here is a case where this does hold."
9639	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, ``Functoriality for quasi-coherent modules''.}
9640	  Let \(\varphi : X \cong Y\) be an isomorphism of schemes and \(H : X.\mathrm{Modules}\) a
9641	  quasi-coherent module. Then the pushforward \(\Phi.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\, H\) along
9642	  \(\varphi\) is again quasi-coherent.
9643	\end{lemma}
9644	\begin{proof}
9645	  \uses{lem:pushforward_commutes_restriction, lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib,
9646	    lem:presentation_map_mathlib, lem:isQuasicoherent_of_coversTop_mathlib,
9647	    lem:nonempty_quasicoherentData_mathlib, lem:presentation_ofIsIso_mathlib,
9648	    lem:isAffineOpen_image_of_iso_mathlib}
9649	  Quasi-coherence is a \emph{local} datum: it transports across the equivalence \(\Phi\) by moving
9650	  \(H\)'s local presentations to the image cover, member by member. We never seek a global presentation
9651	  of \(H\) (it has none — \(H\) is quasi-coherent only on the abstract source); we move the local
9652	  presentations one open at a time.
9653	
9654	  \emph{The cover.} Extract \(H\)'s quasi-coherence datum
9655	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:nonempty_quasicoherentData_mathlib}): a family \((U_i)_{i \in I}\) of opens covering
9656	  \(X\) together with, for each \(i\), a presentation \(p_i\) of \(H.\operatorname{over} U_i\). Form the
9657	  image cover \(V_i = \varphi.\mathrm{inv}^{-1}\,U_i\) of \(Y\); it covers \(Y\) because the underlying
9658	  map of \(\varphi.\mathrm{inv}\) is a homeomorphism, so the preimages of a cover form a cover (the image
9659	  of an affine open under the scheme isomorphism is again affine,
9660	  Lemma~\ref{lem:isAffineOpen_image_of_iso_mathlib}, when one wants the cover to remain affine). By
9661	  Lemma~\ref{lem:isQuasicoherent_of_coversTop_mathlib} it suffices to show that
9662	  \((\Phi.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\, H).\operatorname{over} V_i\) is quasi-coherent for every \(i\).
9663	
9664	  \emph{Per-member transport.} Fix \(i\) and let
9665	  \(e_i : (X.\mathrm{Modules}\!\restriction\! U_i) \simeq (Y.\mathrm{Modules}\!\restriction\! V_i)\) be
9666	  the opens-site equivalence induced by \(\varphi\) restricted to \(U_i\), built from
9667	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence_mathlib}. This \(e_i\) preserves colimits and carries
9668	  the structure-sheaf unit to the structure-sheaf unit (it is a site equivalence whose underlying base
9669	  is a homeomorphism), so by Lemma~\ref{lem:presentation_map_mathlib} the presentation \(p_i\) of
9670	  \(H.\operatorname{over} U_i\) transports to a presentation of
9671	  \(e_i.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}(H.\operatorname{over} U_i)\). The comparison isomorphism of
9672	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_commutes_restriction},
9673	  \[
9674	    e_i.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\bigl(H.\operatorname{over} U_i\bigr)
9675	      \;\cong\;
9676	    \bigl(\Phi.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\, H\bigr).\operatorname{over} V_i,
9677	  \]
9678	  then carries that presentation, via Lemma~\ref{lem:presentation_ofIsIso_mathlib}, to a presentation of
9679	  \((\Phi.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\, H).\operatorname{over} V_i\). A sheaf of modules with a (global)
9680	  presentation is quasi-coherent, so each member of the image cover is quasi-coherent, and the claim
9681	  follows by Lemma~\ref{lem:isQuasicoherent_of_coversTop_mathlib}.
9682	
9683	  This is the isomorphism case of the Stacks functoriality remark (``it is in general not the case that
9684	  the pushforward of a quasi-coherent module is quasi-coherent''; here it holds because \(\varphi\) is an
9685	  isomorphism, equivalently \(\varphi_* = (\varphi^{-1})^*\) is a pullback).
9686	\end{proof}

AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/AbsoluteCohomology.lean:50:noncomputable def jShriekOU_homEquiv (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.Modules) :

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.PresheafCech
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.FreePresheafComplex
9	
10	/-!
11	# Form-B absolute cohomology `H^p(U, F) := Ext^p(jShriekOU U, F)` (P5b input)
12	-/
13	
14	universe u
15	
16	open CategoryTheory Limits CategoryTheory.Abelian
17	
18	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
19	
20	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
21	
22	/-- The standard `HasExt` structure on `X.Modules`, made a section-local instance so the
23	`Ext`-based absolute cohomology below resolves it without the slow `HasSmallLocalizedHom`
24	typeclass search. -/
25	noncomputable local instance hasExtModules : HasExt.{u + 1, u, u + 1} X.Modules :=
26	  HasExt.standard _
27	
28	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Form-B absolute cohomology -/
29	
30	noncomputable def jShriekOU (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : X.Modules :=
31	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).obj
32	    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj U))
33	
34	/-- The sheafification adjunction hom-bijection as an `AddEquiv` (additivity holds because
35	the right adjoint is an additive functor and composition is bilinear). -/
36	noncomputable def sheafificationHomAddEquiv (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.Modules) :
37	    (jShriekOU U ⟶ F) ≃+
38	      ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj U) ⟶
39	        (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) :=
40	  { (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).homEquiv
41	      ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj U)) F with
42	    map_add' := by
43	      intro f g
44	      haveI : (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
45	          PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).Additive := inferInstance
46	      simp only [Equiv.toFun_as_coe, Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]
47	      erw [Functor.map_add, Preadditive.comp_add]
48	      rfl }
49	
50	noncomputable def jShriekOU_homEquiv (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.Modules) :
51	    (jShriekOU U ⟶ F) ≃+
52	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj F).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op U) :=
53	  (sheafificationHomAddEquiv U F).trans (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv U _)
54	
55	/-- **Form-B absolute cohomology** `H^p(U, F) := Ext^p_{O_X}(jShriekOU U, F)`. -/
56	noncomputable def absoluteCohomology (p : ℕ) (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
57	    (F : X.Modules) : AddCommGrpCat :=
58	  AddCommGrpCat.of (Ext (jShriekOU U) F p)
59	
60	/-- **`H⁰(U, F) ≅ Γ(U, F)`** as abelian groups: degree-zero `Ext` out of the
61	corepresenting object `jShriekOU U` recovers the sections of `F` over `U`. This is the
62	additive corepresentability of global sections specialised to `p = 0`. -/
63	noncomputable def absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
64	    (F : X.Modules) :
65	    Ext (jShriekOU U) F 0 ≃+
66	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj F).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op U) :=
67	  (AddEquiv.mk' Ext.homEquiv₀ (by
68	    intro a b
69	    refine Ext.homEquiv₀.symm.injective ?_
70	    simp only [Ext.homEquiv₀_symm_apply, Ext.mk₀_add, Ext.mk₀_homEquiv₀_apply])).trans
71	    (jShriekOU_homEquiv U F)
72	
73	/-- **Injective vanishing**: positive-degree absolute cohomology of an injective sheaf of
74	modules vanishes. Direct from `Ext.eq_zero_of_injective` (the injective object is the
75	*second* `Ext` argument, so no restriction of the coefficient sheaf is needed). -/
76	theorem absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_injective (n : ℕ) (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
77	    (I : X.Modules) [Injective I] (e : Ext (jShriekOU U) I (n + 1)) : e = 0 :=
78	  Ext.eq_zero_of_injective e
79	
80	/-- **Covariant `H^p(U,-)` long exact sequence, surjectivity at `H^{n₁}(U, F₁)`.**
81	Thin wrapper around `Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁` at fixed first argument
82	`jShriekOU U`, for a short exact sequence of `O_X`-modules. -/
83	theorem absoluteCohomology_covariant_exact₁ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
84	    {S : ShortComplex X.Modules} (hS : S.ShortExact) {n₁ : ℕ}
85	    (x₁ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₁ n₁) (hx₁ : x₁.comp (Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero n₁) = 0)
86	    {n₀ : ℕ} (hn₀ : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
87	    ∃ x₃ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₃ n₀, x₃.comp hS.extClass hn₀ = x₁ :=
88	  Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁ (jShriekOU U) hS x₁ hx₁ hn₀
89	
90	/-- **Covariant `H^p(U,-)` long exact sequence, exactness at `H^n(U, F₂)`.**
91	Thin wrapper around `Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₂` at fixed first argument
92	`jShriekOU U`. -/
93	theorem absoluteCohomology_covariant_exact₂ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
94	    {S : ShortComplex X.Modules} (hS : S.ShortExact) {n : ℕ}
95	    (x₂ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₂ n) (hx₂ : x₂.comp (Ext.mk₀ S.g) (add_zero n) = 0) :
96	    ∃ x₁ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₁ n, x₁.comp (Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero n) = x₂ :=
97	  Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₂ (jShriekOU U) hS x₂ hx₂
98	
99	/-- **Covariant `H^p(U,-)` long exact sequence, exactness at the connecting map.**
100	Thin wrapper around `Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₃` at fixed first argument
101	`jShriekOU U`. -/
102	theorem absoluteCohomology_covariant_exact₃ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
103	    {S : ShortComplex X.Modules} (hS : S.ShortExact) {n₀ : ℕ}
104	    (x₃ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₃ n₀) {n₁ : ℕ} (hn₁ : n₀ + 1 = n₁)
105	    (hx₃ : x₃.comp hS.extClass hn₁ = 0) :
106	    ∃ x₂ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₂ n₀, x₂.comp (Ext.mk₀ S.g) (add_zero n₀) = x₃ :=
107	  Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₃ (jShriekOU U) hS x₃ hn₁ hx₃
108	
109	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — naturality of `H⁰ ≅ Γ` -/
110	
111	/-- **Naturality of `Ext.homEquiv₀` in the second argument.** The degree-zero `Ext`
112	comparison intertwines composition with the degree-zero Ext class `Ext.mk₀ g` and ordinary
113	post-composition with `g`. Project-local: Mathlib has the comparison `Ext.homEquiv₀` and the
114	composite formula `Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀`, but not this intertwining as a packaged lemma. -/
115	private lemma homEquiv₀_comp_mk₀ {A B Z : X.Modules} (e : Ext A B 0) (g : B ⟶ Z) :
116	    Ext.homEquiv₀ (e.comp (Ext.mk₀ g) (add_zero 0)) = Ext.homEquiv₀ e ≫ g := by
117	  conv_lhs => rw [← Ext.mk₀_homEquiv₀_apply e, Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀]
118	  rw [← Ext.homEquiv₀_symm_apply, Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
119

238	/-- The sections functor `Γ(V, -) : X.Modules ⥤ Ab` over a fixed open `V`, as the composite of
239	the inclusion of sheaves of modules into presheaves of modules, the forgetful to presheaves of

118	@[simp] lemma Hom.comp_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (ψ : N ⟶ K) : (φ ≫ ψ).app U = φ.app U ≫ ψ.app U := rfl

123	  ext U x
124	  exact congr($(H U.unop) x)

134	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (toPresheaf X), NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
135	  simp [Opposite.op_surjective.forall]

144	end Presheaf

146	noncomputable section Functorial

169	
170	/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes
171	is left adjoint to the pushforward functor. -/
172	def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback f ⊣ pushforward f :=
173	  SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction _
174	
175	section
176	
177	attribute [local instance] preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryCoproducts
178	  preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryProducts

181	instance : (pushforward f).IsRightAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isRightAdjoint
182	instance : (pushforward f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _
183	instance : (pullback f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _
184	
185	end

191	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

193	@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_hom_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
194	@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_inv_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
195

215	@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_inv_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
216

218	identify to the pullback for the composition. -/
219	def pullbackComp :

224	def pushforwardCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pushforward f ≅ pushforward g :=
225	    pushforwardNatIso _ (Opens.mapIso _ _ (hf ▸ rfl)) ≪≫

229	    ((pushforwardCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl

314:  homEquiv_comp {Y Y' : C} (g : Y ⟶ Y') (f : X ⟶ Y) :

310	/-- The data which expresses that a functor `F : C ⥤ Type v` is corepresentable by `X : C`. -/
311	structure CorepresentableBy (F : C ⥤ Type v) (X : C) where
312	  /-- the natural bijection `(X ⟶ Y) ≃ F.obj Y`. -/
313	  homEquiv {Y : C} : (X ⟶ Y) ≃ F.obj Y
314	  homEquiv_comp {Y Y' : C} (g : Y ⟶ Y') (f : X ⟶ Y) :
315	    homEquiv (f ≫ g) = F.map g (homEquiv f) := by cat_disch
316	
317	lemma CorepresentableBy.homEquiv_symm_comp {F : C ⥤ Type v} {X : C}
318	    (e : F.CorepresentableBy X) {Y Y' : C} (y : F.obj Y) (g : Y ⟶ Y') :
319	    e.homEquiv.symm y ≫ g = e.homEquiv.symm (F.map g y) :=
320	  e.homEquiv.injective (by simp [homEquiv_comp])

322	/-- If `F ≅ F'`, and `F` is corepresentable, then `F'` is corepresentable. -/
323	def CorepresentableBy.ofIso {F F' : C ⥤ Type v} {X : C} (e : F.CorepresentableBy X)
324	    (e' : F ≅ F') :
325	    F'.CorepresentableBy X where
326	  homEquiv {X} := e.homEquiv.trans (e'.app _).toEquiv

328	    dsimp
329	    rw [e.homEquiv_comp]
330	    apply e'.hom.naturality_apply

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `jShriekOU` transport along a scheme iso (Need #1)

The corepresenting object `jShriekOU V` of `Γ(V,-)` is carried along a scheme isomorphism
`φ : X ≅ Y` by the induced pushforward equivalence: `Φ.functor (jShriekOU V) ≅ jShriekOU (φ.inv⁻¹V)`.
The argument is pure corepresentability transport — `jShriekOU V` is never unfolded.  Both sides
corepresent the *same* `Type`-valued sections functor `Γ(φ.inv⁻¹V,-)`: the right side directly (the
additive corepresentability `jShriekOU_homEquiv`), the left side via the pushforward adjunction
(`Φ.functor ⊣ Φ.inverse`) composed with the definitional identity "sections of a pushforward =
sections over the preimage open".  `CorepresentableBy.uniqueUpToIso` then produces the iso. -/

/-- **`jShriekOU V` corepresents the `Type`-valued sections functor `Γ(V,-)`.**  Repackages the
additive corepresentability `jShriekOU_homEquiv` as a `CorepresentableBy` of `sectionsFunctor V`
post-composed with the forgetful functor to types. Project-local. -/
private def sectionsCorep {X : Scheme.{u}} (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
    (sectionsFunctor V ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat).CorepresentableBy (jShriekOU V) where
  homEquiv {B} := (jShriekOU_homEquiv V B).toEquiv
  homEquiv_comp {B B'} g f := jShriekOU_homEquiv_nat V f g

/-- **The pushforward of `jShriekOU V` corepresents `Γ(φ.inv⁻¹V,-)`.**  Via the pushforward
adjunction `Φ.functor ⊣ Φ.inverse` and the definitional identity "sections of `φ.inv`-pushforward
over `V` = sections over `φ.inv⁻¹V`", the object `Φ.functor (jShriekOU V)` corepresents the same
`Type`-valued sections functor as `jShriekOU (φ.inv⁻¹V)`.  Project-local. -/
private def sectionsCorepPushforward {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y)
    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
    (sectionsFunctor (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ V) ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat).CorepresentableBy
      ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj (jShriekOU V)) where
  homEquiv {B} :=
    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).toAdjunction.homEquiv (jShriekOU V) B).trans
      (jShriekOU_homEquiv V ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).inverse.obj B)).toEquiv
  homEquiv_comp {B B'} g f := by
    set Φ := Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ with hΦ
    show jShriekOU_homEquiv V _ (Φ.toAdjunction.homEquiv (jShriekOU V) B' (f ≫ g)) = _
    rw [Φ.toAdjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right, jShriekOU_homEquiv_nat V _ (Φ.inverse.map g)]
    rfl

/-- **A scheme isomorphism carries `jShriekOU` along** (blueprint `lem:jshriek_transport_along_iso`).
For `φ : X ≅ Y` and an open `V ⊆ X`, the pushforward equivalence sends the corepresenting object of
`Γ(V,-)` to that of `Γ(φ.inv⁻¹V,-)`:
`Φ.functor (jShriekOU V) ≅ jShriekOU (φ.inv⁻¹V)`.  Project-local: the geometric naturality of `j_!`
under a scheme iso (the first-`Ext`-argument transport of Need #1). -/
lemma jShriekOU_transport_along_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y)
    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj (jShriekOU V) ≅
      jShriekOU (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ V) :=
  (sectionsCorepPushforward φ V).uniqueUpToIso (sectionsCorep (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ V))

512	
513	`qcoh_finite_presentation_cover` (Stacks 01I8, Route B step B1): for a quasi-coherent `F` on
514	`Spec R`, the local quasi-coherence datum (`QuasicoherentData`) refines to a *finite* standard
515	cover `D(g_j) ⊆ U_{φ(j)}` with `span{g_j} = ⊤`, each member `U_{φ(j)}` carrying a presentation of
516	`F.over U_{φ(j)}`.  This is the entry point of the keystone descent: per `g_j` the attached
517	presentation restricts (B2) and bridges (B3/B4) to a global presentation of the affine restriction,
518	which the `IsLocalizedModule` local model then consumes.
519	
520	`coversTop_iSup_eq_top` is the topological translation feeding `exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover`:
521	the cover-of-terminal-object condition of a `QuasicoherentData` on the opens-Grothendieck-topology
522	of a space is the honest `⨆ U_i = ⊤`. -/
523	
524	section FinitePresentationCover
525	
526	/-- A family of opens that covers the terminal object of the opens-Grothendieck-topology of a space
527	has supremum `⊤`.  Project-local: translates the categorical `J.CoversTop` condition carried by a
528	`QuasicoherentData` into the lattice-theoretic `⨆ = ⊤` that `exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover`
529	consumes. -/
530	private lemma coversTop_iSup_eq_top {T : Type*} [TopologicalSpace T] {I : Type*}
531	    (Y : I → TopologicalSpace.Opens T)
532	    (hY : (Opens.grothendieckTopology T).CoversTop Y) :
533	    ⨆ i, Y i = ⊤ := by
534	  rw [eq_top_iff]
535	  intro x _
536	  rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup]
537	  obtain ⟨U, f, hf, hU⟩ := hY ⊤ x (TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_top x)
538	  obtain ⟨i, ⟨g⟩⟩ := hf
539	  exact ⟨i, (leOfHom g) hU⟩
540	
541	/-- **Finite presentation cover from quasi-coherence (Stacks 01I8, Route B step B1).**  For a
542	quasi-coherent `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F`, there is a quasi-coherence datum `q` (a cover `q.X` of
543	`Spec R` together with a presentation of `F.over (q.X i)` for each `i`) and a *finite* standard
544	refinement of it: finitely many `g : Fin n → R` with `span (range g) = ⊤` and indices
545	`φ : Fin n → q.I` such that each basic open `D(g j)` lies inside the cover member `q.X (φ j)`.  The
546	presentation of `F.over (q.X (φ j))` carried by `q` is what steps B2–B4 restrict to `D(g j)`.
547	Project-local: packages Mathlib's `QuasicoherentData` (local generation) with the affine finite
548	basic-open refinement (`exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover`) in the exact form the Route B keystone
549	descent consumes. -/
550	lemma qcoh_finite_presentation_cover (F : (Spec R).Modules)
551	    [hF : F.IsQuasicoherent] :
552	    ∃ (q : SheafOfModules.QuasicoherentData.{u, u, u, u} F)
553	      (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (φ : Fin n → q.I),
554	      (∀ j, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g j) ≤ q.X (φ j)) ∧ Ideal.span (Set.range g) = ⊤ := by
555	  obtain ⟨q⟩ := hF.nonempty_quasicoherentData
556	  have htop : ⨆ i, q.X i = ⊤ := coversTop_iSup_eq_top q.X q.coversTop
557	  obtain ⟨n, g, φ, hgU, hspan⟩ := exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover q.X htop
558	  exact ⟨q, n, g, φ, hgU, hspan⟩

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/OpenImmersionPushforward.lean

## Summary
- **Declarations added (4, axiom-clean):** `jShriekOU_transport_along_iso` (public),
  `sectionsCorep`, `sectionsCorepPushforward` (private helpers), all under the new section
  "`jShriekOU` transport along a scheme iso (Need #1)" (lines ~350–395).
- **Sorries closed:** the `case hjt` sorry in `higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic` (the
  `jShriekOU` transport iso). **Sorry count in file: 3 → 2.**
- **Declarations blocked (1):** `case hqc` of `higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic`
  (pushforward-along-iso preserves quasi-coherence) — genuine large blocker, precise handoff below.
- Pre-existing `_comp` sorry (line ~574) untouched (downstream, not this lane).
- **Verification:** `lake build` exit 0; `#print axioms AlgebraicGeometry.jShriekOU_transport_along_iso`
  = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` (kernel-only).

## `jShriekOU_transport_along_iso` (line ~391) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Statement:** `(pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj (jShriekOU V) ≅ jShriekOU (φ.inv ⁻¹ᵁ V)`
  for `φ : X ≅ Y`, `V : Opens X`. Matches blueprint `lem:jshriek_transport_along_iso`
  (`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.jShriekOU_transport_along_iso}` — already a build target, NOT new debt).
- **Approach (clean, no naturality bookkeeping):** Both `Φ.functor (jShriekOU V)` and
  `jShriekOU (φ.inv⁻¹V)` corepresent the **same** `Type`-valued functor
  `sectionsFunctor (φ.inv⁻¹V) ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat`; apply
  `CategoryTheory.Functor.CorepresentableBy.uniqueUpToIso`.
  - `sectionsCorep V` : the right side, repackaging the additive corepresentability
    `jShriekOU_homEquiv V B` as a `CorepresentableBy`; naturality field is exactly the file's
    `jShriekOU_homEquiv_nat`.
  - `sectionsCorepPushforward φ V` : the left side, `homEquiv` = pushforward adjunction
    `Φ.toAdjunction.homEquiv ∘ jShriekOU_homEquiv V (Φ.inverse.obj B)`. The target type
    `↥Γ(Φ.inverse.obj B, V) = ↥Γ(B, φ.inv⁻¹V)` holds by `rfl` (Mathlib `pushforward_obj_obj`).
    Naturality: `Equiv.trans_apply` → `Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right` → `erw [jShriekOU_homEquiv_nat …]`
    (the `erw` matches through the `.toEquiv` coercion and closes the goal — no trailing `rfl`).
- **Key `rfl` facts verified:** `pushforward φ.inv ⋙ sectionsFunctor V = sectionsFunctor (φ.inv⁻¹V)`
  and `(sectionsFunctor (φ.inv⁻¹V) ⋙ forget).obj B = (sectionsFunctor V ⋙ forget).obj (pushforward φ.inv |>.obj B)`.
- **Wired:** `case hjt => exact jShriekOU_transport_along_iso U.isoSpec (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)`.

## `case hqc` (pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh) — NOT ADDED (blocked, alternatives exhausted)
- **Goal:** `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent ((pushforwardEquivOfIso U.isoSpec).functor.obj H)`
  (general form: `φ : X ≅ Y`, `H : X.Modules` qcoh ⟹ `Φ.functor.obj H` qcoh on `Y`).
  Blueprint `lem:pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh` (`AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh`).
- **The blocker (precise):** the **only** non-circular route is the blueprint's
  `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop` route, whose load-bearing sub-lemma is
  `pushforward_commutes_restriction` (blueprint `lem:pushforward_commutes_restriction`):
  ```
  e_i.functor.obj (H.over U_i)  ≅  (Φ.functor.obj H).over V_i
  ```
  for `V_i = φ.inv⁻¹U_i` and `e_i` the homeomorphism-induced restricted-module-category equivalence.
  This is a genuine ~100–150 LOC site-equivalence lemma: `Scheme.Modules.over V` is
  `SheafOfModules.pushforward (𝟙)` over the **slice site** `Over V`, so the commutation is the
  `pushforwardComp`/`pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` iso for the commuting square of continuous
  functors `Over U_i → Opens X`, `Over V_i → Opens Y`, `φ`. **No Mathlib lemma provides this
  pushforward/`over` commutation** (checked `PushforwardContinuous.lean`, `Quasicoherent.lean`).
- **Alternatives tried and REJECTED (do NOT retry):**
  - **Mathlib direct qcoh-preservation under pullback/pushforward at the Scheme.Modules level:**
    does not exist (only `tilde` carries an `IsQuasicoherent` instance; `QuasicoherentData.ofIsIso`
    is iso-of-the-same-object only).
  - **Tilde shortcut (`N ≅ tilde(Γ N)` ⟹ qcoh):** CIRCULAR — `N.fromTildeΓ` iso ⟺ `IsQuasicoherent N`
    (the 01I8 theorem `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_quasicoherent`), so producing qcoh this way begs the question.
  - **Global-presentation transport (affine U ⟹ H has a global `Presentation`, push along the
    colimit-preserving equivalence):** the "affine ⟹ global presentation" step is itself
    `N ≅ tilde(M)`, same circularity; on `U` (not literally `Spec`) it routes back through the iso.
- **Decomposition for next iter (in `\uses` order):**
  1. `pushforward_commutes_restriction` (blueprint `lem:pushforward_commutes_restriction`) — the iso
     above. Build from `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` (Mathlib) for the commuting
     site square + `Scheme.Modules.pushforward_obj_obj` (rfl on sections).
  2. A cover-transport helper: `(Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).CoversTop (fun i => φ.inv⁻¹ (U i))`
     from `(Opens.grothendieckTopology X).CoversTop U` (φ homeomorphism). Self-contained; can reuse the
     pattern of `QcohTildeSections.coversTop_iSup_eq_top` (converse direction).
  3. `pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh` = `of_coversTop` with cover (2), each member discharged by
     transporting `H`'s local presentation (`nonempty_quasicoherentData`) along (1) via
     `Presentation.map`/`Presentation.ofIsIso` + `Presentation.isQuasicoherent`.
  - Then `case hqc => exact pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh U.isoSpec H hH` (general φ form), and the
    `_acyclic` leaf closes completely.

## Why I stopped
- **Real progress:** 1 public axiom-clean declaration (`jShriekOU_transport_along_iso`, line ~391)
  + 2 private axiom-clean helpers (`sectionsCorep` ~363, `sectionsCorepPushforward` ~372); closed
